title: Discovering Available Layers
text: Load firex. packages needed catalog browsing.
code: library(firex)

title: Discovering Available Layers
text: wri_overview() reads local STAC index prints structured summary 82 available layers. Call arguments see domains, dimensions, data types glance: object summarizes 82 available layers grouped domain, dimension, data type.
code: wri_overview() WRI DATA SUMMARY ------------------- Read at:      2026-05-11 16:07:50  Layer ID Naming Convention: wri_domain + wri_dimension + data_type  Collections (1): wri_ignitR  Unique wri_domain values (11): air_quality, communities, iconic_places, iconic_species, infrastructure, livelihoods, natural_habitats, sense_of_place, species, unknown, water  Unique data_type values (3): aggregate, final_score, indicator  Unique wri_dimension values (5): domain_score, recovery, resilience, resistance, status  Total layers available: 82

what: Getting the Catalog as a Data Frame
title: Discovering Available Layers
text: wri_overview_df() returns information flat data frame, one row per layer. useful filtering standard R tools: ten named WRI domains air_quality, communities, iconic_places, iconic_species, infrastructure, livelihoods, natural_habitats, sense_of_place, species, water. unknown domain used overall WRI_score layer. five WRI dimension values domain_score, recovery, resilience, resistance, status.
code: df <- wri_overview_df() nrow(df) #> [1] 82  # Filter to water-domain aggregate layers subset(   df,   wri_domain == "water" & data_type == "aggregate",   select = c(id, wri_dimension, data_type) ) #>                    id wri_dimension data_type #> 75 water_domain_score  domain_score aggregate #> 76   water_resilience    resilience aggregate #> 77   water_resistance    resistance aggregate #> 80       water_status        status aggregate

title: Discovering Available Layers
text: identified layer ID, layer_info() retrieves full metadata record, including hosted COG URL, geographic extent, CRS, classification properties. Transposing t() makes easier read: asset_href field direct URL hosted COG file KNB. get_layer() uses URL open virtual connection via GDAL; need use directly. xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax fields give layer’s geographic extent WGS84 (EPSG:4326).
code: t(layer_info("WRI_score")) #>            WRI_score #> id         WRI_score #> collection wri_ignitR #> asset_href https://knb.ecoinformatics.org/.../WRI_score.tif #> proj_code  EPSG:5070 #> data_type  final_score #> wri_domain unknown #> is_hosted  TRUE #> xmin       -146.2082 #> ymin        19.1074 #> xmax        173.7109 #> ymax         54.8056

title: Discovering Available Layers
text: filtering combinations properties easily expressed data frame queries, query_stac_flexible() filters underlying STAC item list property value: users identify layer ID, can pass ID get_layer() use existing spatial object area interest.
code: catalog <- wri_overview() items <- lapply(catalog$data$items, function(x) x$item)  # All resistance indicators in the water domain water_resist <- query_stac_flexible(   items,   wri_domain = "water",   wri_dimension = "resistance",   data_type = "indicator" )  vapply(water_resist, function(x) x$id, character(1)) #> [1] "water_resistance_drought_plans" #> [2] "water_resistance_water_treatment"

title: Retrieving a Layer by Bounding Box
code: library(firex) library(terra)

title: Retrieving a Layer by Bounding Box
text: get_layer() takes layer ID, aoi argument, numeric bounding boxes explicit aoi_crs. Supply bounding box c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax). function checks overlap layer extent returns terra raster COG native projection.
code: # Santa Barbara County, CA in WGS84 sb <- c(-120.7, 34.4, -119.4, 35.1)  wri <- get_layer("WRI_score", aoi = sb, aoi_crs = "EPSG:4326") wri #> class       : SpatRaster #> dimensions  : 482, 756, 1  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 90, 90  (x, y) #> coord. ref. : NAD83 / Conus Albers (EPSG:5070) #> name        : WRI_score

title: Retrieving a Layer by Bounding Box
text: Figure 4. WRI score bounding box Santa Barbara County, CA, returned get_layer(). Darker red values indicate higher WRI score values.
code: plot(   wri,   main = "WRI Score -- Santa Barbara County",   col = hcl.colors(100, "YlOrRd", rev = TRUE) )

title: Retrieving a Layer by Bounding Box
text: get_layer() returns standard SpatRaster, full terra ecosystem immediately available analysis:
code: # Summary statistics global(wri, fun = c("mean", "sd", "min", "max"), na.rm = TRUE) #>    mean    sd  min   max #>   0.531 0.112 0.18 0.894  # Write to disk as a GeoTIFF writeRaster(wri, "wri_score_sb.tif")  # Reproject to WGS84 for export to other tools wri_wgs84 <- project(wri, "EPSG:4326")

title: Retrieving a Layer by Bounding Box
text: aoi_crs accepts format terra recognizes: integer EPSG code, character string, PROJ WKT string. equivalent:
code: get_layer("WRI_score", aoi = sb, aoi_crs = 4326) get_layer("WRI_score", aoi = sb, aoi_crs = "EPSG:4326") get_layer("WRI_score", aoi = sb, aoi_crs = "WGS84")

what: Retrieving Multiple Layers for the Same Area
title: Retrieving a Layer by Bounding Box
text: Use lapply() iterate vector layer IDs. layers share extent resolution retrieved, results can stacked directly terra::c():
code: water_ids <- subset(   wri_overview_df(),   wri_domain == "water" & data_type == "aggregate" )$id  water_layers <- lapply(   water_ids,   get_layer,   aoi = sb,   aoi_crs = "EPSG:4326" )  names(water_layers) <- water_ids  stack <- do.call(c, water_layers) plot(stack, col = hcl.colors(100, "YlOrRd", rev = TRUE))

title: Retrieving a Layer by Bounding Box
text: bounding box extends partly outside COG coverage area, get_layer() returns overlapping portion issues warning naming sides overflowed: bounding box falls entirely outside covered area, function stops informative error making network request: Use polygon boundary place rectangular bounding box already study area saved spatial object file.
code: wri_big <- get_layer(   "WRI_score",   aoi = c(-130, 30, -55, 55),   aoi_crs = "EPSG:4326" ) #> Warning: Requested bbox extends outside the layer extent to the east. tryCatch(   get_layer(     "WRI_score",     aoi = c(-80, -90, -60, -70),     aoi_crs = "EPSG:4326"   ),   error = function(e) message("Error: ", conditionMessage(e)) ) #> Error: Requested bbox does not overlap the layer extent.

title: Using Spatial Objects as Areas of Interest
code: library(firex) library(terra) library(sf)

title: Using Spatial Objects as Areas of Interest
text: get_layer() accepts several area--interest input types, including numeric bounding boxes, spatial files, sf objects, terra objects. Inputs without stored coordinate reference system information, numeric bounding boxes SpatExtent objects, require aoi_crs.

title: Using Spatial Objects as Areas of Interest
text: Pass file path character string. shapefile, coordinate reference system read .prj sidecar file, aoi_crs needed. returned raster covers extent Eaton Fire perimeter. avoids manually entering coordinates keeping result compatible terra workflows.
code: shp <- system.file(   "demos/data/Eaton_Perimeter_20250121.shp",   package = "firex" )  wri_eaton <- get_layer("WRI_score", aoi = shp)  wri_eaton #> class       : SpatRaster #> dimensions  : 193, 256, 1  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 90, 90  (x, y) #> coord. ref. : NAD83 / Conus Albers (EPSG:5070)

title: Using Spatial Objects as Areas of Interest
text: shows users can choose input format best matches workflow. package handles spatial object derives retrieval extent internally.
code: eaton <- sf::st_read(shp, quiet = TRUE) wri_sf <- get_layer("WRI_score", aoi = eaton)  # The shapefile path and sf object define the same area of interest. all.equal(terra::ext(wri_eaton), terra::ext(wri_sf)) #> [1] TRUE

title: Using Spatial Objects as Areas of Interest
text: terra::SpatExtent stores bounding coordinates, coordinate reference system. , aoi_crs must supplied explicitly. Figure 5. Polygon-based area--interest input. Left: Eaton Fire perimeter bounding extent derived geometry. Right: get_layer() retrieves WRI_score raster extent, original perimeter overlaid context. figure generated reproducible script inst/figures/fig5_bbox_vs_polygon.R.
code: eaton_v <- terra::vect(shp) eaton_crs <- terra::crs(eaton_v) eaton_ext <- terra::ext(eaton_v)  wri_ext <- get_layer(   "WRI_score",   aoi = eaton_ext,   aoi_crs = eaton_crs )

title: Access Wildfire Resilience Index Data
text: Access Western Wildfire Resilience Index R without downloading full dataset. firex connects 82 wildfire resilience raster layers hosted Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFFs (COGs) Knowledge Network Biocomplexity (KNB). Users can browse catalog, inspect layer metadata, retrieve spatial subsets bounding box polygon boundary — local storage full archive (~25 GB) required.

title: Access Wildfire Resilience Index Data
code: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("ignitR-package/firex")

title: Access Wildfire Resilience Index Data
code: library(firex) library(terra)  # Browse all 82 available layers wri_overview()  # Get metadata for a specific layer layer_info("WRI_score")  # Retrieve the WRI composite score for Santa Barbara County, CA wri <- get_layer("WRI_score",                   aoi     = c(-120.7, 34.4, -119.4, 35.1),                   aoi_crs = "EPSG:4326")  plot(wri, col = hcl.colors(100, "YlOrRd", rev = TRUE))

title: Access Wildfire Resilience Index Data
text: get_layer() accepts numeric bounding boxes, shapefile GeoJSON file paths, sf objects, SpatVector, SpatRaster, SpatExtent aoi. inputs without embedded CRS (numeric vectors SpatExtent), supply aoi_crs.

title: Access Wildfire Resilience Index Data
text: man/ inst/ follow standard R package conventions required build system. man/ generated roxygen2 edited directly.
code: firex/ ├── R/                    # Package functions ├── man/                  # Auto-generated documentation (do not edit) ├── inst/ │   ├── stac/             # Bundled STAC catalog, collection, and item JSONs │   └── demos/            # Demo data and example scripts ├── tests/testthat/       # Unit tests ├── DESCRIPTION           # Package metadata and dependencies ├── NAMESPACE             # Exported functions (auto-generated) └── README.md

title: Access Wildfire Resilience Index Data
text: WWRI dataset produced NCEAS funded Gordon Betty Moore Foundation. COG archive hosted KNB https://knb.ecoinformatics.org/data/wri-data-processing/cogs/. persistent DOI assigned following formal publication underlying dataset. dataset documentation, methodology, interactive dashboard, visit official Western Wildfire Resilience Index site.

title: Access Wildfire Resilience Index Data

what: Check whether a bounding box falls within a layer extent — check_extent_overlap
title: Check whether a bounding box falls within a layer extent — check_extent_overlap
text: Checks whether requested bounding box fully contained within extent layer bounding box.

title: Check whether a bounding box falls within a layer extent — check_extent_overlap
code: check_extent_overlap(bbox, layer_bbox)

title: Check whether a bounding box falls within a layer extent — check_extent_overlap
text: bbox Numeric vector form c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax) EPSG:4326. layer_bbox Numeric vector form c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax) describing layer extent EPSG:4326.

title: Check whether a bounding box falls within a layer extent — check_extent_overlap
text: length-1 logical. Returns TRUE bbox fully   contained within requested layer extent FALSE otherwise.   return value carries "status", "message",   "bbox" attributes can inspected later.

title: Check whether a bounding box falls within a layer extent — check_extent_overlap
text: Overlap outcomes: Full overlap - returns TRUE Partial overlap - returns FALSE message describing   side layer extent bbox exceeds overlap - returns FALSE message bbox   overlap layer extent

what: Get a WRI raster layer — get_layer
title: Get a WRI raster layer — get_layer
text: Retrieves hosted WRI raster asset optionally crops spatial area interest.

title: Get a WRI raster layer — get_layer
code: get_layer(id, aoi = NULL, aoi_crs = NULL)

title: Get a WRI raster layer — get_layer
text: id Character. Layer id retrieve. See [wri_overview_df()] available ids. aoi Optional area interest used crop layer. Accepted inputs: `NULL`, return full layer. Numeric vector `c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax)`. Character file path readable `terra::vect()` `terra::rast()`, shapefile, GeoJSON, GeoPackage, raster file. `terra` objects: `SpatVector`, `SpatRaster`, `SpatExtent`, `SpatVectorProxy`, `SpatVectorCollection`, `SpatRasterCollection`, `SpatRasterDataset`. `sf` `sfc` objects. `sp` spatial objects. `raster` package objects: `RasterLayer`, `RasterStack`, `RasterBrick`, `Extent`. `bbox` object matrix accepted `terra::ext()`. CRS-less AOIs, numeric bounding boxes extents, require `aoi_crs`. aoi_crs Optional character integer CRS specification `aoi`, `"EPSG:4326"` `4326`.

title: Get a WRI raster layer — get_layer
text: [terra::SpatRaster] cropped `aoi`, full layer   `aoi = NULL`.

what: Get metadata for one WRI layer — layer_info
title: Get metadata for one WRI layer — layer_info
text: Returns metadata single layer WRI STAC catalog.

title: Get metadata for one WRI layer — layer_info
code: layer_info(layer_id, fresh = TRUE)

title: Get metadata for one WRI layer — layer_info
text: layer_id Character. layer/item id look , e.g. `"WRI_score"`. fresh Logical. TRUE (default), rebuilds catalog disk. FALSE, implemented yet.

title: Get metadata for one WRI layer — layer_info
text: one-row data frame containing metadata requested layer.

what: Print a WRI overview summary — print.wri_overview
title: Print a WRI overview summary — print.wri_overview
text: Prints compact summary collections layer metadata `wri_overview` object.

title: Print a WRI overview summary — print.wri_overview
code: # S3 method for class 'wri_overview' print(x, ...)

title: Print a WRI overview summary — print.wri_overview
text: x `wri_overview` object. ... Additional arguments passed S3 method. Currently unused.

title: Print a WRI overview summary — print.wri_overview
text: Called side effect printing summary.

what: Query STAC items flexibly by any property — query_stac_flexible
title: Query STAC items flexibly by any property — query_stac_flexible
text: Query STAC items flexibly property

title: Query STAC items flexibly by any property — query_stac_flexible
code: query_stac_flexible(items, ...)

title: Query STAC items flexibly by any property — query_stac_flexible
text: items List STAC items ... Named property-value pairs filter

title: Query STAC items flexibly by any property — query_stac_flexible
text: Filtered list items

title: Query STAC items flexibly by any property — query_stac_flexible
code: items <- list(   list(properties = list(wri_domain = "water", data_type = "status")),   list(properties = list(wri_domain = "land", data_type = "status")) ) query_stac_flexible(items, wri_domain = "water", data_type = "status") #> [[1]] #> [[1]]$properties #> [[1]]$properties$wri_domain #> [1] "water" #>  #> [[1]]$properties$data_type #> [1] "status" #>  #>  #>

what: Resolve an area of interest — resolve_aoi
title: Resolve an area of interest — resolve_aoi
text: Converts supported area--interest inputs `terra` polygon, bounding box, extent, CRS.

title: Resolve an area of interest — resolve_aoi
code: resolve_aoi(aoi, aoi_crs = NULL)

title: Resolve an area of interest — resolve_aoi
text: aoi Area interest. Accepted inputs include `NULL`, numeric vector `c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax)`, character file path readable `terra::vect()` `terra::rast()`, object supported `terra::ext()`. aoi_crs Optional character integer CRS specification `aoi`, `"EPSG:4326"` `4326`.

title: Resolve an area of interest — resolve_aoi
text: length-1 logical result metadata stored attributes,   including `"aoi"`, `"bbox"`, `"extent"`, `"crs"`, `"type"`,   `"message"` validation fails.

title: Resolve an area-of-interest CRS — resolve_aoi_crs
text: Validates supplied CRS checks whether area--interest object already carries one.

title: Resolve an area-of-interest CRS — resolve_aoi_crs
code: resolve_aoi_crs(aoi, aoi_crs = NULL)

title: Resolve an area-of-interest CRS — resolve_aoi_crs
text: aoi Area interest inspect existing CRS. aoi_crs Optional character integer CRS specification, `"EPSG:4326"` `4326`.

title: Resolve an area-of-interest CRS — resolve_aoi_crs
text: length-1 logical resolved CRS stored `"crs"`   attribute success, validation message stored `"message"`   failure.
